歩いて.mnemonics: Visualize 'a-row-ee-te' as someone saying 'I row, eat' while walking around.

～で.mnemonics: Think of 'de' as someone saying 'day' as the method of doing something.

どうやって.mnemonics: Imagine 'doh yaht-te' as someone asking 'dough, yacht?' to figure out a solution.

どのぐらい.mnemonics: Visualize 'dono-gurai' as someone asking 'don't know, gray?' to estimate an amount.

～週刊.mnemonics: Think of 'shuu-kan' as someone saying 'shoe can' for a weekly publication.

～か月.mnemonics: Imagine 'ka-gatsu' as someone saying 'cactus' to refer to a number of months.

～年.mnemonics: Visualize 'nen' as someone saying 'men' to refer to a number of years.

このごろ.mnemonics: Think of 'kono goro' as someone saying 'corn, oh goro!' to talk about recent times.

～後.mnemonics: Imagine 'go' as someone shouting 'go!' to indicate an event or time after something.

～か～.mnemonics: Visualize 'ka' as someone asking 'car?' to offer choices between two options.

普通.mnemonics: Visualize 'futsuu' as a regular person ('futsuu') taking the local train.

急行.mnemonics: Think of 'kyuukou' as a train ('kyuukou') rushing past stations.

特急.mnemonics: Remember 'tokkyuu' as a special ('tokkyuu') train offering fast service.

～生き.mnemonics: Visualize 'iki' as a train bound for a specific destination.

～方面.mnemonics: Think of 'houmen' as a train heading in a specific direction.

乗車券.mnemonics: Remember 'joushaken' as a ticket to board ('jousha') the train.

往復.mnemonics: Think of 'oufuku' as going ('ou') and coming back ('fuku').

出口.mnemonics: Remember 'deguchi' as the way ('deguchi') out of the station.

入口.mnemonics: Think of 'iriguchi' as the way ('iriguchi') into the station.
